message:
test(ft-geg03): no-mocks webhook tests + LocalHttpServer fixture

Builds the no-mocks parallel suite for webhook_labruntime.rs against
a hermetic local HTTP server, completing the labruntime no-mocks
trilogy alongside ft-dvgzi (snapshot fixture) and ft-2funa (watchdog
+ kill_mux fault injection).

Files:
- tests/common/local_http_server.rs (~220 LOC, std-net only):
  hermetic HTTP/1.1 server bound to 127.0.0.1:0, serves canned
  responses (200/4xx/5xx/drop-connection/stall), captures the
  request body+headers the dispatcher actually sent on the wire.
  Zero new workspace deps.
- tests/webhook_real_http.rs (4 tests): drives a real
  WebhookDispatcher against the local server using a small inline
  RealHttpTransport (std::net::TcpStream + raw HTTP/1.1).

Tests:
- real_http_dispatch_succeeds_against_local_server — 200 OK on
  real POST; server captures the request body and confirms the
  rendered summary actually traversed the wire (not just a mock
  return value)
- real_http_dispatch_records_failure_on_500 — server returns 500
  → dispatcher records accepted=false, status_code=500
- real_http_dispatch_records_failure_on_connection_drop — server
  resets the connection without responding → DeliveryResult::err
  surfaces from the transport's parse_status path
- real_http_dispatch_records_failure_on_unreachable_port — bind
  127.0.0.1:0, drop the listener, attempt connect → ECONNREFUSED
  surfaces as DeliveryResult::err

## Architectural finding (worth documenting)
WebhookTransport is a pub trait in frankenterm-core/src/webhook.rs
with NO concrete real-HTTP implementer in the workspace —
production code that uses WebhookDispatcher injects its own
transport. The labruntime suite's MockTransport is the only impl
the test crate sees. This means the no-mocks audit had to ship its
own real-HTTP transport just to exercise the trait against actual
HTTP framing. The transport mirrors the convention used by the
labruntime suite's MockTransport::failure helper (4xx/5xx →
DeliveryResult::err) — without that mapping, a real HTTP client
would silently swallow server errors, which is the very class of
bug the no-mocks suite exists to catch.

Verified: FT_REAL_HTTP_TESTS=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core
--test webhook_real_http --features asupersync-runtime
→ 20 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored. Default cargo test (no env)
takes the skip path: 20 passed, 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
